updated: July 15, 2026 sync. Two things moved. DTE finally has a name and a date: Jon Johnson, Program Manager in Workforce Development, wants to bring Work Readiness on Tuesday August 4 at noon. That day is already our Election Day Voter Hotline, and August 4 was not one of the days you offered him, you offered Wednesday the 5th or Thursday the 6th. So he needs an answer with a real day. Wayne State is also waiting on you. Jessica Archer will not build the July 28 schedule until she knows what the group has already seen on campus and what you want them to get out of the day, and you told her you would send that this morning. Lunch is still unconfirmed on their end. Everything else from the last sync still stands: banking and credit both need a partner, confirm all 15 are on the GDYT Approved for Placement list, Payroll 2 timesheets are still not live in myOneFlow so keep tracking hours by hand, and YPC registration is due Monday July 20.
headline: Week 2, Financial Literacy, is underway (opened Monday July 13). Huntington backed out of Banking 101 this year, so both the banking and credit sessions still need a partner. YNAB with Jermaine is set. DTE is back in with Jon Johnson on Work Readiness, but he picked August 4, which collides with the Voter Hotline, so send him a day. Wayne State needs your answers before they finish the July 28 campus day. CPR is approved and locked for Thursday July 30. GDYT: our youth are Payroll 2, first pay July 31, pay cards pick up Thursday July 23 by appointment.

week1.tag: No power at the Branch after Friday's storm
week1.note: Canceled. Orientation moves to Wednesday. Everyone still gets paid for this day. Families were notified the night of July 6.

week1.tag: Done. Orientation plus U.S. DOL, in person 10 to 2
week1.note: Done. This was the first day. Orientation with Jermaine and Je'Tina, the U.S. Department of Labor (Mildred Kress) on wages and workplace rights, then AI Day Part 1 and Part 2.

week1.tag: Done. Workplace Foundations · in person, 10 to 2
week1.note: Done. Day two, in person 10 to 2. Team-building and AI reflection, a built work-readiness session, then Workplace Ethics Court and Time Management.

sessions.activity: Slot a built session (Interview / Job Post / Decode)

week1.note: Done. Virtual and self-paced. Youth submitted their Capstone Kickoff on their own time. This closed out Week 1.

doNow.title: Send Wayne State what you want out of the campus day
doNow.desc: Jessica Archer at WSU will not send the July 28 schedule until she hears back from you. She asked two things: has the group been to Wayne State before, and what do you want them to see or hear, like faculty from a particular field or a specific program. You told her July 14 you would ask the young people and send it over the next morning. Several of them have been on campus in past years, so she wants to build on that instead of repeating it. She is still working out lunch and will confirm.

doNow.title: Give DTE a day that actually works
doNow.desc: Monique handed BRIDGES to Jon Johnson, a Program Manager in Workforce Development at DTE, and he is in. He is asking to bring Work Readiness on Tuesday August 4 at 12:00. Two problems: August 4 is our Election Day Voter Hotline, and it was not one of the days you offered him. You offered Wednesday August 5 or Thursday August 6. August 5 may go to the Young Professionals Conference, and Darryl has 11:00 on August 6, so noon on the 6th is the clean answer if you want both. Write him back with a day.

doNow.title: Cover the banking side of Week 2 (Huntington out)
doNow.desc: Courtney Elledge at Huntington wrote back and cannot do Banking 101 this year. She offered to send financial education and banking materials for the youth to use during the week. So now both Banking 101 and the credit session still need a plan. Take her materials, and either find a fast replacement or have Jermaine run Banking 101. Week 2 is already underway, so this needs a plan now.

doNow.title: Price CPR for staff and youth council
doNow.desc: Kamilia approved the CPR and First Aid cert for all 15 youth (Natosha Gurley, RN, $12 each, about $180). She now wants the total cost to also certify staff and some youth council members. Natosha is open to more people. Get a headcount and send Kamilia the number.

doNow.title: Confirm all 15 are on the final GDYT approved list
doNow.desc: Nicole Hill's July 14 update says the Youth Enrollment Tracker now holds the final Approved for Placement list, and only youth on it can work or be paid. Youth who worked before being approved cannot be paid for that time, and GDYT will not cover unapproved youth. Check the YET and confirm all 15 of ours show Approved for Placement. Also get the worksite ready for GDYT monitor visits over the next few weeks: original work permits on hand and a first aid kit.

doNow.title: Get the 15 youth into the GDYT Empower App
doNow.desc: Kelsey at Connect Detroit sent the app sign-up reminder with a youth instructions deck. The app's modules also satisfy the GDYT financial literacy requirement, and it's separate from payroll. Connect Detroit will send weekly emails showing who still needs to register, so get everyone in early.

doNow.title: Decide on the Young Professionals Conference (register by July 20)
doNow.desc: Connect Detroit (Shuna Hayward) sent the 2026 Young Professionals Conference details. It moved from July 23 to Wednesday August 5, 8:30 to 2:00 at Huntington Place, sponsored by DWIHN for the summer youth employment programs. Free, with breakfast, lunch, breakout sessions and a resource fair. Registration for the org, youth and chaperones is due Monday July 20, and space is first come first served. August 5 is open in our Week 5, so this could be the youth's field day that week. Note that August 5 is also one of the days you offered DTE, so pick one. Decide if we are going and register.

doNow.title: Lock the rest of Week 4
doNow.desc: CPR is locked for Thursday July 30 (approved). Still need Keosha's health day (7/29 or 7/30) and Zipporah's Financial Wellness date.

doNow.title: Answer the two Week 3 virtual requests
doNow.desc: A brother and sister both emailed July 12 asking to work virtually July 20 to 29, and they are fully out July 21 to 24. That covers the in-person heart of Week 3 (Dream Car Reveal Tue 7/21, Entrepreneur Panel Thu 7/23). Reply to work out a virtual plan for those days so they stay on payroll and don't fall behind on the Capstone.

doNow.title: Confirm the two bounced families are set for the rest
doNow.desc: Two addresses bounced on the July 6 all-family email. Week 1 is done. Confirm both families by text or call that they have the schedule for the remaining weeks. One of the two also has a working gmail on the same thread.

Register the org, youth and chaperones by Monday July 20, first come first served. Free, and it fits the open Aug 5 slot in our Week 5.
